name: Moroccan Chickpea Stew
intro: This recipe came to be as a way to use the kale that was flourishing in the garden. It could also incorporate other vegetables - whatever is in your fridge that needs to be used. Serve over couscous.

ingredients.item: (14.5 ounces) of can diced tomatoes, undrained
(14.5 ounces) of can chickpeas, drained and rinsed

method: Heat the olive oil in a large pot over medium heat; cook the onion and garlic in the hot oil until the onions are translucent, 5 to 7 minutes. Stir the cumin, coriander, cayenne pepper, garam masala, curry powder, and salt into the onion and garlic; cook together until fragrant, about 1 minute. Add the potatoes, diced tomatoes, tomato sauce, and raisins to the pot. Pour enough water over the mixture to cover; bring to a simmer and cook until the potatoes are soft, 10 to 15 minutes.
Add the chickpeas and kale to the pot; simmer until the kale wilts, about 3 minutes. Sprinkle the cilantro over the stew and immediately remove the pot from the heat.
